features.name: Project scaffold
features.notes: Git repo initialized with .gitignore, .env.example, and docker-compose.yml per foundation plan Task 1. Directories created. No sensors applicable yet — repo has no application code. NEXT_PUBLIC_BETTER_AUTH_URL intentionally added early as a harmless placeholder needed by the Better Auth client config in Task 7. QA approved with reservations; required corrections completed. Security verdict: ADVISORY only. Follow-ups: runtime rejection of placeholder secrets and broader env-file ignore coverage before deployment work.

features.name: Nginx config
features.notes: Nginx reverse-proxy config at nginx/nginx.conf. Proxies / to next:3000 with proper headers. FastAPI not publicly routed. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Residual risk: local nginx syntax validation deferred until tooling/runtime is available.

features.name: PostgreSQL schema migration
features.notes: Initial schema created at database/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql. 5 tables: words, cards, review_log, ai_content_cache, user_settings with CHECK, UNIQUE, REFERENCES constraints and 3 indexes for hot query paths. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Follow-ups: enforce review_log.user_id consistency with cards.user_id; define cleanup for soft Better Auth user references; verify FSRS due serialization; later decide provider allowlist and AI cache expiry policy.

features.name: Hebrew seed data and import script
features.notes: Top-20 Biblical Hebrew words in database/seed/words.csv with idempotent importer (seed_words.py). Uses ON CONFLICT (hebrew) DO NOTHING against UNIQUE hebrew column, parameterized queries, DATABASE_URL from env. Default CSV path resolved relative to script location. Connection cleanup via try/finally. Initial QA rejected dead-code idempotency; correction added UNIQUE words.hebrew plus ON CONFLICT (hebrew), fixed the duplicated erets gloss, made the default CSV path script-relative, and added explicit cleanup. QA recheck: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN.

features.name: FastAPI stub
features.notes: Minimal FastAPI stub at backend/. Health check only. Dependencies: fastapi==0.136.1, uvicorn[standard]==0.46.0. Dockerfile uses python:3.14-slim. LiteLLM excluded per ADR-001. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Container runtime validation deferred because Docker Compose is unavailable locally.

features.name: Next.js scaffold
features.notes: Next.js 16.2.6 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui (button/card/input/label). Better Auth deps installed. Dockerfile uses node:24-alpine. output: standalone. Build and lint green. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Pre-Task 7 follow-ups: remove or justify @better-auth/client pre-alpha dependency, move shadcn to devDependencies, update frontend/CLAUDE.md to reference root contract, and consider non-root runner user before deployment.

features.name: Better Auth configuration
features.notes: Foundation plan Task 7. Entry preconditions from foundation-006 review: audit/remove @better-auth/client if unnecessary, move shadcn to devDependencies, and repair frontend/CLAUDE.md root-contract linkage before auth implementation.

open_decisions: Confirm the initial Hebrew seed list size and source before production import.
Confirm the production AI provider priority order behind the internal provider adapter.
Confirm final VPS hostname and certificate issuance details before deploy work.
Decide how to enforce review_log.user_id consistency with cards.user_id before production data paths rely on it.
Before foundation-007, confirm whether @better-auth/client is needed at all; if not, remove the pre-alpha package and use the stable better-auth/client sub-path from the main package.
